title: Post-Quantum Cryptography Beyond TLS: Remain Quantum Safe | Akamai
why:
- Post-quantum cryptography (PQC) is now being delivered in TLS 1.3 handshakes for both client-to-Akamai and Akamai-to-origin connections, with features being rolled out as default for all Enhanced TLS customers.
- The quantum threat affects all areas of cryptography that use asymmetric key algorithms (such as RSA and Diffie–Hellman) and digital signatures (such as ECDSA), not just TLS.
- Many critical protocols and services—such as SMTP, IMAP, POP, LDAP, VPNs, and XMPP—use TLS for encryption and authentication, making it necessary to upgrade them to post-quantum/traditional (PQ/T) hybrid key exchange algorithms like X25519MLKEM768.
- OpenSSH, the most widely used SSH implementation, has supported the Streamlined NTRU Prime (SNTRUP) algorithm in hybrid configurations since 2021 and, as of version 9.9, defaults to the ML-KEM (FIPS 203) plus X25519 ECDH hybrid key exchange.
- The latest OpenSSH client versions (10.1 and above) provide active warnings if connecting to servers that do not use post-quantum key agreement, helping users to identify potentially vulnerable connections.
